rulings.Case Title: SHAIR VS ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BHC)*
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. AT DEPOSITION PG. 2404, PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT HE DOES NOT REMEMBER WHAT DEFENDANT WAS DOING AT THE CONTRA COSTA SITE. THE REASONABLE INFERENCE FROM THIS ADMISSION IS THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT KNOW WHETHER DEFENDANT EXPOSED HIM TO ASBESTOS. THIS EVIDENCE IS SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN UNDER CCP 437C(O)(2). UNDER D'AMICO, THE COURT STRIKES PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION BECAUSE IT CONTRADICTS HIS DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. PARAGRAPH 5 OF THE DECLARATION FAILS TO PROVIDE A SUFFICIENT EXPLANATION FOR THE CONTRADICTION. SPECIFICALLY, THE DECLARATION FAILS TO EXPLAIN PRECISELY HOW PLAIINTIFF'S FRIENDS AND FAMILY MEMBERS JOGGED HIS MEMORY TO REMEMBER ALL OF THE DETAILS HIGHLIGHTED IN PARAGRAPH 8 OF THE DECLARATION. (302/AJR/PB)
